answer: The population of Vietnam in 2024 is estimated at 101.3 million people. This projection is based on the results of the 2024 census and aligns with the country's demographic trends. The population is expected to continue growing, reaching 100.9 million by mid-year.

results.title: Vietnam Population (1950-2025) - Macrotrends
results.snippet: ## Vietnam Population (1950-2025). Population Economy Trade Health Education Development Labor Force Environment Crime Immigration Other. Population Growth Rate Density Urban Rural Life Expectancy Birth Rate Death Rate Infant Mortality Rate Fertility Rate. ##### Total current population for Vietnam in 2025 was **100,103,979**, a **0.61% increase** from 2024. * Total population for Vietnam in 2024 was **99,497,680**, a **0.85% decline** from 2023. * Total population for Vietnam in 2023 was **100,352,192**, a **0.67% increase** from 2022. * Total population for Vietnam in 2022 was **99,680,655**, a **0.75% increase** from 2021. Total population is based on the de facto definition of population, which counts all residents regardless of legal status or citizenship. The values shown are midyear estimates. The full historical dataset is available for download here: Population of Vietnam | Historical Data | 1950 - 2025.

results.title: Viet Nam is third populous country in Southeast Asia
results.snippet: SOCIALIST REPUBLIC OF VIET NAM. # Viet Nam is third populous country in Southeast Asia. ## VGP - As of April 1, 2024, Viet Nam’s population reached 101,112,656 people, ranking third in the Southeast Asia and 16th in the world, the General Statistics Office reported on January 6. * #### Viet Nam projected to become aging population society by 2038. * #### Viet Nam’s population exceeds 100 million mark. Between 2019 and 2024, Viet Nam's population increased by 4.9 million people. The population density of Viet Nam was approximately 305 people per square kilometer. These results positioned Viet Nam as the third most densely populated country in Southeast Asia, behind the Philippines and Singapore. The country is experiencing a golden population structure, with the share of the population aged between 15 and 64 years making up 67.4 percent. Viet Nam's total fertility rate (TFR) in 2024 was around 1.91 children per woman. ONLINE NEWSPAPER OF THE GOVERNMENT OF THE SOCIALIST REPUBLIC OF VIET NAM.
